source: Study guide for Exam AB-100: Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect — Microsoft Learn (effective July 22, 2026)
note: Official exam has 3 domains. Platform splits official Domain 3 (Deploy 40-45%) into D3 (Deploy/ALM 22%) and D4 (Responsible AI 22%) for study clarity.

tasks.title: Analyze requirements for AI-powered business solutions
knowledge: How agents can be applied to task automation, data analytics, and decision-making scenarios
The characteristics of high-quality grounding data: accuracy, relevance, timeliness, cleanliness, and availability
Methods for organizing and exposing business solution data for consumption by AI systems
The relationship between data quality and AI agent output reliability
skills: Assess the use of agents in task automation, data analytics, and decision-making
Review data for grounding, including accuracy, relevance, timeliness, cleanliness, and availability
Organize business solution data to be available for other AI systems

tasks.title: Design overall AI strategy for business solutions
knowledge: The AI adoption process stages in the Cloud Adoption Framework for Azure
When to use prebuilt agents vs. custom agents vs. extending Microsoft 365 Copilot
When to create custom AI models vs. using foundation models via Copilot Studio or Microsoft Foundry
The elements of the Microsoft AI Center of Excellence
How knowledge sources and generative AI orchestration work in Copilot Studio agents
Prompt engineering guidelines and techniques applicable to business solutions
How customized small language models (SLMs) serve specific business use cases
Architectural considerations for AI solutions that span multiple Dynamics 365 apps
skills: Implement the AI adoption process from the Cloud Adoption Framework for Azure
Design the strategy for building AI and agents in business solutions
Design a multi-agent solution by using platforms such as Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, and Microsoft Foundry
Develop the use cases for prebuilt agents in the solution
Define the solution rules and constraints when building AI components with Copilot Studio, Microsoft Foundry and Foundry Tools
Determine the use of generative AI and knowledge sources in agents built with Copilot Studio
Determine when to build custom agents or extend Microsoft 365 Copilot
Determine when custom AI models should be created
Provide guidelines for creating a prompt library
Develop the use cases for customized small language models for the solution
Provide prompt engineering guidelines and techniques for AI-powered business solutions
Include the elements of the Microsoft AI Center of Excellence
Design AI solutions that use multiple Dynamics 365 apps

tasks.title: Evaluate the costs and benefits of an AI-powered business solution
knowledge: ROI criteria and total cost of ownership (TCO) components for AI business solutions
The build vs. buy vs. extend decision framework for AI components
How model routers work and when intelligent routing reduces cost or improves quality
How to quantify AI business value in terms measurable by stakeholders
skills: Select ROI criteria for AI-powered business solutions, including the total cost of ownership
Create an ROI analysis for the proposed AI solution for a business process
Analyze whether to build, buy, or extend AI components for business solutions
Implement a model router to intelligently route requests to the most suitable model

tasks.title: Design AI and agents for business solutions
knowledge: The differences between task agents, autonomous agents, and prompt-and-response agents
Copilot Studio topic design including fallback topics for unrecognized intents
When to use NLP, conversational language understanding (CLU), or generative AI orchestration in Copilot Studio
How to design data processing pipelines for AI model grounding
How to embed AI components in Power Apps canvas apps
The Microsoft Power Platform Well-Architected Framework for intelligent workloads
Agent flows and prompt actions in Copilot Studio
Copilot customizations for Dynamics 365 Customer Experience and Service apps
skills: Design business terms for Copilot in Dynamics 365 apps for customer experience and service
Design customizations of Copilot in Dynamics 365 apps for customer experience and service
Design connectors for Copilot in Dynamics 365 Sales
Design agents for integration with Dynamics 365 Contact Center channels
Design task agents
Design autonomous agents
Design prompt and response agents
Propose Foundry Tools for a given requirement
Propose code-first generative pages and the use of an agent feed for apps
Design topics for Copilot Studio, including fallback
Design data processing for AI models and grounding
Design a business process to include AI components in a Power Apps canvas app
Apply the Microsoft Power Platform Well-Architected Framework to intelligent application workloads
Determine when to use standard natural language processing, conversational language understanding, or generative AI orchestration in Copilot Studio
Design agents and agent flows with Copilot Studio
Design prompt actions in Copilot Studio

tasks.title: Design extensibility of AI solutions
knowledge: How to extend agents in Copilot Studio using Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Computer Use capability in Copilot Studio for automating tasks in apps and websites
How agent behaviors (reasoning, voice mode) are configured in Copilot Studio
Custom model design and deployment in Microsoft Foundry
Agent extensibility options in Microsoft 365 Copilot
How Microsoft 365 agents integrate with Teams and SharePoint
skills: Design AI solutions by using custom models in Microsoft Foundry
Design agents in Microsoft 365 Copilot
Design agent extensibility in Copilot Studio
Design agent extensibility with Model Context Protocol in Copilot Studio
Design agents to automate tasks in apps and websites by using Computer Use in Copilot Studio
Design agent behaviors in Copilot Studio, including reasoning and voice mode
Optimize solution design by using agents in Microsoft 365, including Teams and SharePoint

tasks.title: Orchestrate configuration for prebuilt agents and apps
knowledge: AI features available in Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain Management
AI features available in Dynamics 365 Customer Experience and Service apps
Microsoft 365 Copilot for Sales and Copilot for Service configuration options
Microsoft Power Platform AI capabilities including AI Hub
How to add knowledge sources to Dynamics 365 Finance and Supply Chain in-app help
Agent-to-agent (A2A) interoperability and knowledge source federation
skills: Orchestrate AI features in Dynamics 365 apps for finance and supply chain
Orchestrate AI features in Dynamics 365 apps for customer experience and service
Propose Microsoft 365 agents for business scenarios
Orchestrate the configuration of Microsoft 365 Copilot for Sales and Microsoft 365 Copilot for Service
Propose Microsoft Power Platform AI features, including AI hub
Design interoperability of the finance and operations agent chats to use additional knowledge sources
Recommend the process of adding knowledge sources to in-app help and guidance for Dynamics 365 Finance or Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management apps

tasks.title: Analyze, monitor, and tune AI-powered business solutions
knowledge: Monitoring tools for AI agents: Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Azure AI Evaluation
Key agent performance metrics: latency, error rate, groundedness, relevance, coherence
How telemetry data is used to detect model drift and performance degradation
How to analyze user feedback and backlog data to identify agent improvement opportunities
AI-based tools for issue analysis and automated tuning suggestions
skills: Recommend the process and tools required for monitoring agents
Analyze backlog and user feedback of AI and agent usage
Apply AI-based tools to analyze and identify issues and perform tuning
Monitor agent performance and metrics
Interpret telemetry data for performance and model tuning

tasks.title: Manage the testing of AI-powered business solutions
knowledge: Evaluation metrics for AI agents: groundedness, relevance, coherence, safety, task completion
Validation criteria frameworks for custom AI models
Effective Copilot prompt best practices and how to validate them
End-to-end test scenario design for multi-app Dynamics 365 solutions
How Copilot can assist in generating and managing test cases
skills: Recommend the process and metrics to test agents
Create validation criteria of custom AI models
Validate effective Copilot prompt best practices
Design end-to-end test scenarios of AI solutions that use multiple Dynamics 365 apps
Build the strategy for creating test cases by using Copilot

tasks.title: Design the ALM process for AI-powered business solutions
knowledge: ALM for Copilot Studio: Power Platform solutions, pac CLI, environment strategy, connection references
ALM for Microsoft Foundry Agents: agent-as-code, deployment pipelines, Key Vault for secrets
ALM for custom AI models: versioning, model registry, blue-green deployments, rollback
ALM for data in AI models: data versioning, lineage tracking, ETL pipeline governance
ALM patterns for AI in Dynamics 365 Finance, Supply Chain, and Customer Experience apps
Canary deployment and promotion gates for AI solutions
skills: Design the ALM process for data used in AI models and agents
Design the ALM process for Copilot Studio agents, connectors, and actions
Design the ALM process for Microsoft Foundry Agents service
Design the ALM process for custom AI models
Design the ALM process for AI in Dynamics 365 apps for finance and supply chain
Design the ALM process for AI in Dynamics 365 apps for customer experience and service

domains.title: Responsible AI & Lifecycle Governance
domains.officialWeight: Tested within Domain 3 (Deploy 40-45%) on the official AB-100 exam

tasks.title: Design responsible AI, security, governance, risk management, and compliance
knowledge: Microsoft Responsible AI principles: Fairness, Reliability & Safety, Privacy & Security, Inclusiveness, Transparency, Accountability
Azure AI Content Safety capabilities: content filtering, prompt shields, groundedness detection
EU AI Act risk classification tiers and obligations (minimal, limited, high-risk, prohibited)
GDPR principles applicable to AI systems: storage limitation, data minimization, right to erasure
Prompt injection attack types (direct and indirect) and mitigation strategies
Network isolation patterns: private endpoints, VNet integration, Azure Private DNS
Access control patterns for grounding data: pre-retrieval filtering, Entra ID on-behalf-of flow
Immutable audit trail architectures: Azure Blob WORM policy, retention policies
Model decommissioning and the challenge of right-to-erasure in fine-tuned models
Shared responsibility model for AI solutions on Azure
skills: Design security for agents
Design governance for agents
Design model security
Analyze solution and AI vulnerabilities and mitigations, including prompt manipulation
Review solution for adherence to responsible AI principles
Validate data residency and movement compliance
Design access controls on grounding data and model tuning
Design audit trails for changes to models and data
